D10 Imaging L1 Identify Atom

Shutter: Freeze or Smear

a short shutter freezes the moment; a long one paints the subject as a trail of where it was during the exposure

The shutter decides how long the sensor sees the scene. Open it for a tiny fraction of a second and the world is frozen — a bird's wings, a rain drop, a sprinter mid-stride. Open it for a long fraction and anything that moves smears across the frame as a trail of light, while still things stay sharp.

Drag the shutter from a thousandth of a second to a quarter second. The flying subject snaps from a single sharp ball to a long blur tracking the path it took. The background, sitting still, stays sharp at every speed.
